title: phelipanche cohenii
intro: Phelipanche cohenii, also known as Cohen's broomrape, is a parasitic plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and ability to obtain nutrients from the surrounding host plants. The plant has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities. Traditionally, Phelipanche cohenii has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and is believed to offer benefits in reducing inflammation and improving overall well-being. In terms of herbal preparations, Phelipanche cohenii can be consumed as a decoction, made by boiling the roots or leaves in water, or used in the form of a tincture or infusion.
